refsection.title: Description
refsection.para: This is a client library for accessing SOAP services of ISDS (Informační systém datových schránek / Data Box Information System) as defined in Czech ISDS Act (300/2008 Coll.) and implied documents. Current implementation details are described in Provozní řád that can be downloaded from Dokumenty ke stažení section of ISDS Information Portal.

The library provides a C language interface with synchronous non-reentrant blocking calls. Network communication progress reporting and operation logging and library debugging are implemented by calling back application-provided functions. Network operations can be canceled from network reporting call-back.

refsection.title: Library Initialization and Deinitialization
refsection.para: A libisds application must include isds.h header file. The application must call isds_init function to initialize the library before calling any other library functions. After last libisds call, isds_cleanup function should be called to clean up some global resources and to deinitialize dependent libraries.

refsection.title: Contexts
refsection.para: Most of the functions operate on an established connection to the ISDS server. This is called a context and it's represented by a pointer to an opaque isds_ctx structure. The structure maintains state about network connection, authorization or error from last call on the context.
The context is allocated by isds_ctx_create function and deallocated by isds_ctx_free function.

There are more context subtypes. A specific subtype is assigned to the context when a fresh new context is passed to one of the few stratifying functions (isds_login, czp_convert_document, isds_request_new_testing_box). Once the context is specialized, you can use it only with functions understanding the subtype. This is not enforced by the library now, but it does not matter much because all the other functions assume the isds_login was called on the context. In other words, do not share the context among the three stratifying functions.

For example create a context with isds_ctx_create, then call isds_login, then work with box, then call isds_logout. Here you can reuse the context and log in as another user by calling isds_login again or destroy the context with isds_ctx_free if you don't need it anymore.

Or create a context with isds_ctx_create, send a document to authorized conversion using czp_convert_document, then you can send more documents to the authorized conversion by calling czp_convert_document again on the same context and finally destroy the context with isds_ctx_free.

refsection.title: Errors
refsection.para: Most of the functions return an error code of isds_error type. IE_SUCCESS value denotes a successful call. Other values represent some kind of failure.
You can use isds_strerror function to obtain a human readable string representation of the error code.
If a function with context argument failed, you can use isds_long_message function to obtain a detailed error message. Please note that returned value lasts only to the next call on the context.

refsection.title: Character Encoding
refsection.para: All strings exchanged between the library and the application are encoded in UTF-8. Although there are a few exceptions:

listitem.para: isds_strerror and isds_long_message functions return locale encoded string.
isds_version returns locale encoded string.
Log call-back function set by isds_set_log_callback function is called with raw byte stream.
isds_pki_credentials structure string members have encoding specific to cryptographic library linked to cURL library.

refsection.title: Global Settings
refsection.para: Some functions influence library behavior globally. These are:

listitem.para: isds_init and isds_cleanup used to initialize and deinitialize the library.
isds_set_logging and isds_set_log_callback to set logging.

refsection.title: Logging and Debugging
refsection.para: Logging is global for all libisds calls. Log level and facility can be set with isds_set_logging function.
The log is printed on standard error output by default. Application can redirect the messages to a call-back function by registering the call-back function with isds_set_log_callback.

refsection.title: Network Input/Output
refsection.para: Some functions operating on a context create network sockets and do network input and output.
Network timeout can be set with isds_set_timeout function. Function calls aborted owing to the timeout will return IE_TIMED_OUT.

Network operation progress can be monitored by a call-back function. The call-back function can be registered using isds_set_progress_callback function. Registered call-back function will be called periodically with arguments declaring amount of transferred data. The call-back return value determines whether to continue in the network operation or to cancel the operation. Functions failed owing to canceling network operation will return IE_ABORTED.

refsection.title: Memory management
refsection.para: The library provides destructors for all libisds data structures. For example isds_ctx_free function accepts a pointer to a pointer to the isds_ctx structure, frees the double referenced structure (recursively), writes NULL to the pointed pointer (which invalidates the pointer effectively) and returns nothing.

Upon a function call, all output arguments are automatically reallocated to desired size. On a function failure, all output arguments are automatically deallocated and their pointers set to NULL. Exceptions are documented at respective functions.

Output strings are allocated using standard malloc call. Application is responsible for their deallocation (in case of no failure and if not specified otherwise.) Use standard free call for strings, use libisds destructors for libisds structures.

refsection.title: Available Functions, Types, and Constants
refsection.para: See isds.h header file.

refsection.title: Description
refsection.para: This header file declares inteface for libisds library.

refsection.title: Service locators
refsection.para: Addresses of known ISDS servers.

refsection.title: Base URLs of production ISDS instance

refsection.para: Without client certificate authentication.
With client certificate authentication.
Without OTP authentication.

refsection.title: Base URLs of testing ISDS instance

refsection.para: Without client certificate authentication.
With client certificate authentication.
Without OTP authentication.

refsection.title: struct isds_ctx
refsection.para: Context for specific ISDS box.
Error code. Known values:

listitem.para: No error. Numeric value 0.
Unspecified error.
Operation is not supported.
Invalid value.
The context is not valid.
The context has not been logged in.
Network connection has been closed.
Time limit for network operation exceeded.
Requested entity does exist.
Not enough memory.
Network error.
Error on HTTP level.
Error on SOAP level.
Error on XML level.
Problem with ISDS server.
Invalid enum value.
Invalid date value.
Value is too big.
Value is too small.
Value is not uniq.
Compared values are not equal.
Operaration on a vector succeded for some values, but failed for others.
Operation was aborted by application request.
Security requirements were not satisfied.

refsection.para: Log level. Know values:
Log facility. Know values:

listitem.para: 0xFF

refsection.para: libisds option identifiers. Known values:

listitem.para: Option type is _Bool. Whether to verify server identity. Default value is true.
Option type is char *. Option value is a file name with certificate authority certificates. Default value depends on used cryptographic library.
Option type is char *, Option value is a directory with certificate authority certificates. Default value depends on used cryptographic library.
Option type is char *. Option value is a file name with certificat revocation list in PEM format. Default value depends on used cryptographic library.
Optiona type is _Bool. Whether to normalize MIME type values. Default value is false.

refsection.para: TLS libisds option identifiers. Known values:

listitem.para: Option type is _Bool. Whether to verify server identity.
Option type is char *. Option value is a file name with certificate authority certificates.
Option type is char *. Option value is a directory name with certificate authority certificates.
Option type is char *. Option value is a file name with certificate revocation list in PEM format.

refsection.para: Cryptographic material encoding. Known values:

listitem.para: PEM format.
The material is stored in a cryptographic engine.

refsection.title: struct isds_pki_credentials
refsection.para: This structure holds public key infrastructure cryptographic material to authenticate a client. Members are:

listitem.para: String identifier of cryptographic engine to use (where key is stored). Use NULL for no engine.
Certificate format.

A path to client certificate, or a certificate nickname in case of NSS as curl back-end, or key slot identifier inside cryptographic engine. Some cryptographinc engines can pair certificate with key automatically (NULL value).
Private key format.
A path to client private key, or key identifier in case an engine is used.
Zero terminated string with password for decrypting private key, or engine PIN. Use NULL for no pass-phrase or to let the engine to ask for it.

refsection.para: One-time password authentication method. Known values:

listitem.para: HMAC-based OTP method.
Time-based OTP method.

refsection.para: One-time password authentication resolution. Known values:

listitem.para: Authentication succeded.
Status is unkown.
Bad log-in. You can retry to log in.
Access blocked for 60 minutes. (Because a brute force attack was detected.)
Password has expired.

note.para: It's not clear which password expired: OTP or regular password?

listitem.para: OTP cannot be sent repeatedly at this rate. (Minimal delay depends on TOTP window setting.)
User name is not allowed to access requested URI.
OTP has been generated and sent by the ISDS to the user.
OTP could not been sent by the ISDS. Retry later.

refsection.title: struct isds_otp
refsection.para: This structure holds one-time password when authenticating a client and resolution of the authentication.
Input members are:

listitem.para: Select OTP method to use.
One-time password to use. Pass NULL, if you do not know it yet (e.g. in case of first phase of time-based OTP authentication to request new code from ISDS.)

refsection.para: Output members are:

listitem.para: Fine-grade resolution of this OTP authentication attempt.

refsection.para: Box type. It classifies box owner by his legal status. Known values:

listitem.para: This is a special value for isds_find_box_by_fulltext. It's not accepted by any other services.
This is a special value for sender of messages sent by the ISDS. You can find it only in incomming messages. It's not accepted by any other services.
Standard government (state or municipality or similar) office.
Notary.
Executor.
Subsidiary office with OVM (governing) status assigned on request (section 6 and 7 of the act).
Standard commercial organization (listed in trade registry).
Other organization founded by an act.
An organization with a box assigned on its request.
Person in bussiness.
Lawyer.
Tax consultant.
Administrator of insolvency.
Standard person.

refsection.para: Box status from point of view of accessibility. Known values:

listitem.para: The box is accessible.
The box is temporarily inaccessible.
The box has not yet been activated.
The box is permanently inaccessible.
The box has been removed.

refsection.para: Distinct user permissions from point of view of ISDS. Instances can be bitmaps of any of these distinct values. Distinct known values are:

listitem.para: The user can download and read messages with dmPersonalDelivery equaled to false.
The user can download and read messages with dmPersonalDelivery equaled to true.
The user can create and send messages, the user can download outgoing (sent) messages.
The user can list messages and read data about a message post and delivery.
The user can can search for boxes.
The user can administer his box (to add and remove permitted users and to modify theirs permissions.)
The user can read messages stored in the long term storage.

note.para: This permission is not used since 2012-05.

listitem.para: The user can delete messages from the long term storage.

refsection.para: Message status. Known values are:

listitem.para: The message has been put into ISDS.
Message was stamped by a time stamp authority.
The message included viruses. Infected documents have been removed from the message.
The message was delivered. (dmDeliveryTime is populated.)
The message was delivered through fiction, dmAcceptanceTime is populated.
The message was accepted (by user's log-in or user's explicit request). dmAcceptanceTime is populated.
The message has been read by a user.
The message could not been delivered. (E.g.The recipient's box has been made inaccessible meantime.)
The message's content was deleted.
The message is stored in the long term storage.

refsection.para: The values can be combined into a bit mask for some functions. A special MESSAGESTATE_ANY macro denotes any of the states.
Hash algorithm types. Known values are:

listitem.para: MD5.
SHA-1.

refsection.para: Buffer storage strategy. This type defines how a function should embed application provided buffer into raw element of output structure. Known values are:

listitem.para: Don't fill raw member.
Copy buffer content into newly allocated raw member.
Copy pointer. Leave deallocation to structure destructor (isds_*_free()).

refsection.title: struct isds_hash
refsection.para: This is a hash value storage. Members are:

listitem.para: Hash algorithm.
Hash value length in bytes.
Hash value as a byte stream.

refsection.title: struct isds_PersonName
refsection.para: Name of a person. Members are:

listitem.para: First name.
Middle name.
Current last name.
Last name at birth.

refsection.title: struct isds_BirthInfo
refsection.para: Date and place of a birth. Members are:

listitem.para: Date of birth in local time at the birth place. Only tm_year, tm_mon and tm_mday members of the struct tm carry sane value. Others are undefined.
City where a person was born.
Region where a person was born. This is the kind of region that is called Bezirk in German and okres in Czech.
State wher a person was born.

refsection.title: struct isds_Address
refsection.para: Postal address. Members are:

listitem.para: City.
Street.
Identification of an entrance on the street. Číslo orientační in Czech.
Identification of a building in the municipality. Číslo popisné in Czech.
Postal code for mail routing.
State.

refsection.title: struct isds_DbOwnerInfo
refsection.para: Data about a box and his owner. NULL pointer values mean undefined values. Members are:

listitem.para: Box identifier. Specification limits the length to 7 characters.
Box type.
Identifier of the owner.
Name of a person owning the box.
Name of a firm owning the box.
Birth details of the person.
Postal address of the owner.
Nationality of the owner.
E-mail addres of the owner.
Telephone number of the owner.
External box identifier for data provider (OVM, PO, maybe PFO box types.) Specification limits the length to 20 characters.
PFO external registry code. Specification limits the length to 5 characters.
Box state. 1 means the box is active.

note.para: The type is long int because specification declares it as xsd:integer.

listitem.para: The Box has OVM role (section 5a of the act).

varlistentry.listitem:
This
non-OVM box is free to receive messages from anybody.

refsection.para: User type. Known values are:

listitem.para: Owner of the box.
User with limited access to the box.
User who can manage USERTYPE_ENTRUSTED users.
Company liquidator.

refsection.title: struct isds_DbUserInfo
refsection.para: Data about a user. NULL pointer values mean undefined values. Members are:

listitem.para: User identifier. Specification limits the length from 6 to 12 characters.
User type.
Set of user permissions.
Name of the user.
Postal address of the user.
Date of birth in local time. Only tm_year, tm_mon and tm_mday members of the struct tm carry sane value. Others are undefined.
Identifier a supervising firm. Specification limits the length to 8 characters.
Name of a supervising firm. Specification limits the length to 100 characters.
Contact address. Street and number.
Czech city of the contact address.
Postal code of the contact address.
Abbreviated country of contact address. This value is optional and implicit meaning is CZ.
